title: Inventory Iwad Search And Hash Evidence
summary: Current-state inventory of source-only metadata for the IWAD search and hash evidence surface across three committed launch-lane bootstrap audit ledger modules under src/bootstrap/, three committed playable-contract IWAD runtime modules under src/playable/, and two committed plan_vanilla_parity hash-evidence pin documents that together declare how the codebase searches for an IWAD and which SHA-256 fingerprints anchor IWAD identity for vanilla DOOM 1.9 parity. The bootstrap audit ledgers are src/bootstrap/implement-iwad-discovery-order.ts (505 lines, pins the verbatim seven-candidate access(R_OK) probe order doom2f.wad → doom2.wad → plutonia.wad → tnt.wad → doomu.wad → doom.wad → doom1.wad in id Software linuxdoom-1.10 d_main.c IdentifyVersion, the DOOMWADDIR/`.` fallback rule, the first-match-wins semantics, the gamemode-derived-from-filename rule, and the indeterminate-mode fallback), src/bootstrap/implement-local-shareware-iwad-default-path.ts (594 lines, pins the verbatim '%s/doom1.wad' sprintf format used in the seventh access-block of IdentifyVersion, the lowercase literal probe basename 'doom1.wad' versus the uppercase on-disk basename 'DOOM1.WAD' on case-insensitive Windows hosts, the two project-local gitignored drop directories 'doom' and 'iwad', and the seven-candidate higher-priority count of 6 candidates probed before the shareware default-path block fires), and src/bootstrap/implement-user-supplied-iwad-selection.ts (680 lines, pins the verbatim three developer-mode parameter triple -shdev/-regdev/-comdev, the absence of a vanilla -iwad flag in DOOM 1.9, the developer-mode hardcoded DEVDATA basename trio doom1.wad/doom.wad/doom2.wad, and the early-return bypass that short-circuits the seven-candidate scan whenever a developer-mode parameter matches). The playable-contract runtime modules are src/playable/bun-runtime-entry-point/implementIwadDiscovery.ts (125 lines, pins DEFAULT_LOCAL_IWAD_PATH='doom\\DOOM1.WAD' and the discoverIwadPath() Bun.file().exists() existence-probe contract for the future bun run doom.ts entrypoint with a tri-state DiscoverIwadPathResult discriminated by source 'command-line' | 'default-local-reference-bundle' and status 'discovered' | 'missing'), src/playable/bun-runtime-entry-point/implementMissingIwadError.ts (111 lines, pins the missing-IWAD startup error contract with the canonical messageHeader 'Missing IWAD file for playable launch.', the recoveryText 'Provide --iwad <path-to-iwad> or place DOOM1.WAD at doom\\DOOM1.WAD.', and the failureKind 'missing-iwad'), and src/playable/bun-launch-local-distribution-boundary/verifyIwadDiscoveryAtLaunch.ts (111 lines, pins the launch-time IWAD discovery verification contract with PRODUCT_RUNTIME_COMMAND='bun run doom.ts' and LEGACY_PACKAGE_START_SCRIPT='bun run src/main.ts'). The plan_vanilla_parity hash-evidence pin documents are plan_vanilla_parity/pin-shareware-doom-one-point-nine-primary-target.md (129 lines, pins the shareware DOOM1.WAD SHA-256 1D7D43BE501E67D927E415E0B8F3E29C3BF33075E859721816F652A526CAC771, byte size 4196020, lump count 1264, the DOS executable DOOMD.EXE SHA-256 9D3216605417888D5A699FA3794E46638EB4CB8634501AE748D74F9C2CFAE37B, byte size 709753, the merged Windows executable DOOM.EXE SHA-256 5CA97717FD79F833B248AE5985FFFADBEE39533F5E0529BC9D7B439E08B8F1D2, byte size 1893888, the shareware iwad search relative paths doom/DOOM1.WAD and iwad/DOOM1.WAD, and the proprietary asset redistribution policy that bars publication of the IWAD or executables under any name) and plan_vanilla_parity/pin-user-supplied-registered-and-ultimate-iwad-scope.md (168 lines, pins the user-supplied registered DOOM.WAD and Ultimate DOOM.WAD search relative paths doom/DOOM.WAD and iwad/DOOM.WAD, the registered identification rule E3M1 present and E4M1 absent, the Ultimate identification rule E4M1 present, and the user-supplied-only redistribution policy that bars bundling either DOOM.WAD copy with this repository). Companion tests at test/vanilla_parity/launch/implement-iwad-discovery-order.test.ts (494 lines), test/vanilla_parity/launch/implement-local-shareware-iwad-default-path.test.ts (601 lines), test/vanilla_parity/launch/implement-user-supplied-iwad-selection.test.ts (741 lines), test/playable/bun-runtime-entry-point/implement-iwad-discovery.test.ts (293 lines), test/playable/bun-runtime-entry-point/implement-missing-iwad-error.test.ts (110 lines), test/playable/bun-launch-local-distribution-boundary/verify-iwad-discovery-at-launch.test.ts (82 lines), test/plan_vanilla_parity/pin-shareware-doom-one-point-nine-primary-target.test.ts (438 lines), and test/plan_vanilla_parity/pin-user-supplied-registered-and-ultimate-iwad-scope.test.ts (367 lines) are inventoried for size_bytes, line_count, and sha256 only. The proprietary doom/ and iwad/ reference bundle directories are NOT inventoried here because they lie outside this step's read-only paths and contain proprietary id Software bytes that the redistribution policy bars from any committed file; this inventory captures only the source-level declarations ABOUT the IWAD search surface and the SHA-256 fingerprints that anchor IWAD identity. The reference/manifests/file-hashes.json companion manifest is referenced by filename only because it was already inventoried byte-for-byte by the prior 01-019 inventory-local-doom-reference-files step.
evidence_method: inventory lane local repository audit using readdirSync enumeration of src/bootstrap/, src/playable/bun-runtime-entry-point/, src/playable/bun-launch-local-distribution-boundary/, and plan_vanilla_parity/, statSync for size_bytes, line counting via readFileSync split('\n') with trailing-newline normalization matching the prior 01-019 countLines() helper, sha256sum-equivalent SHA-256 fingerprints over the raw file bytes, regex extraction of `^export (const|function|interface|type|class)` declarations from each TypeScript module, Grep enumeration of importing files to derive src_importer_count and test_importer_count, and JSON-encoded constant capture for the seven-candidate IWAD probe order, the developer-mode parameter triple, the shareware default-path components, the playable-contract runtime contracts, and the pinned shareware-target SHA-256 hashes plus byte sizes plus relative paths. The doom/ and iwad/ proprietary asset directories are intentionally NOT enumerated because they lie outside this step's read-only paths and would imply byte-level enumeration of the proprietary id Software IWAD and executables that the redistribution policy bars from this repository. plan_engine/ and plan_fps/ are listed as read-only paths in the step contract but are NOT enumerated here — their reference-related artifacts are prior art under plan_vanilla_parity governance. The reference/manifests/file-hashes.json file is referenced by filename only because it was already byte-level inventoried by the prior 01-019 step.

source_modules.role: Audit ledger that pins the verbatim seven-candidate access(R_OK) IWAD probe order from id Software linuxdoom-1.10 d_main.c IdentifyVersion. Declares VANILLA_IDENTIFY_VERSION_CONTRACT_AUDIT (7 ledger entries with stable IDs SEARCH_DIRECTORY_IS_DOOMWADDIR_OR_CURRENT_WORKING_DIRECTORY, CANDIDATE_FILENAME_LIST_IS_FIXED, CANDIDATE_PROBE_ORDER_IS_FIXED, FIRST_MATCH_WINS, GAME_MODE_DERIVED_FROM_MATCHED_FILENAME, NO_MATCH_LEAVES_GAME_MODE_INDETERMINATE, CANDIDATE_FILENAMES_ARE_LOWERCASE_LITERALS), VANILLA_IWAD_CANDIDATES (7 frozen entries: doom2f.wad → commercial, doom2.wad → commercial, plutonia.wad → commercial, tnt.wad → commercial, doomu.wad → retail, doom.wad → registered, doom1.wad → shareware), VANILLA_DEFAULT_SEARCH_DIRECTORY='.', VANILLA_INDETERMINATE_MESSAGE='Game mode indeterminate.\\n', VANILLA_IWAD_DISCOVERY_DERIVED_INVARIANTS (7 derived parity invariants), VANILLA_IWAD_DISCOVERY_PROBES (10 pinned probes covering shareware-only-matches-doom1, registered-doom-wad-matches-before-shareware, retail-doomu-wad-matches-before-registered, french-doom2f-matches-before-english-doom2, plutonia-matches-as-commercial, tnt-matches-as-commercial, no-iwad-returns-indetermined, unrelated-wads-leave-game-mode-indeterminate, doomwaddir-set-overrides-current-directory, all-seven-candidates-are-probed-when-no-match), and the REFERENCE_VANILLA_IWAD_DISCOVERY_HANDLER frozen handler with crossCheckVanillaIwadDiscovery and deriveExpectedDiscoveryResult helpers. The module imports only the GameMode type from src/bootstrap/gameMode.ts and exposes no runtime side effects.

Audit ledger that pins the verbatim '%s/doom1.wad' sprintf format from the seventh access-block of id Software linuxdoom-1.10 d_main.c IdentifyVersion plus the project's two gitignored drop locations doom/ and iwad/. Declares VANILLA_SHAREWARE_IWAD_DEFAULT_PATH_CONTRACT_AUDIT (7 ledger entries pinning the lowercase literal probe basename, the doomwaddir-prefixed format, the dot-prefix fallback, the literal shareware enum constant, the seventh-of-seven probe position, the indeterminate-printout fallback on miss, and the Windows-only case-insensitive resolution rule), VANILLA_SHAREWARE_IWAD_VANILLA_PROBE_BASENAME='doom1.wad', VANILLA_SHAREWARE_IWAD_ON_DISK_BASENAME='DOOM1.WAD', VANILLA_SHAREWARE_IWAD_DEFAULT_SEARCH_DIRECTORY='.', VANILLA_SHAREWARE_IWAD_PATH_SEPARATOR='/', VANILLA_SHAREWARE_IWAD_GAME_MODE='shareware', VANILLA_SHAREWARE_IWAD_DEFAULT_FULL_PATH='./doom1.wad', VANILLA_SHAREWARE_IWAD_HIGHER_PRIORITY_CANDIDATE_COUNT=6, VANILLA_SHAREWARE_IWAD_HIGHER_PRIORITY_CANDIDATE_BASENAMES (6 frozen entries doom2f.wad, doom2.wad, plutonia.wad, tnt.wad, doomu.wad, doom.wad), VANILLA_SHAREWARE_IWAD_LOCAL_DROP_DIRECTORIES (2 frozen entries 'doom' and 'iwad'), VANILLA_SHAREWARE_IWAD_LOCAL_DROP_PATHS (2 frozen entries 'doom/DOOM1.WAD' and 'iwad/DOOM1.WAD'), VANILLA_SHAREWARE_IWAD_DEFAULT_PATH_DERIVED_INVARIANTS (9 derived parity invariants), VANILLA_SHAREWARE_IWAD_DEFAULT_PATH_PROBES (10 pinned probes), and the REFERENCE_VANILLA_SHAREWARE_IWAD_DEFAULT_PATH_HANDLER frozen handler with crossCheckVanillaShareWareIwadDefaultPath and deriveExpectedShareWareIwadDefaultPathResult helpers. The module imports only the GameMode type from src/bootstrap/gameMode.ts.

Audit ledger that pins the verbatim three developer-mode parameter triple -shdev/-regdev/-comdev plus the absence of a vanilla -iwad flag from id Software linuxdoom-1.10 d_main.c IdentifyVersion. Declares VANILLA_USER_SUPPLIED_IWAD_SELECTION_CONTRACT_AUDIT (11 ledger entries pinning the fixed-triple parameter list, the shdev-then-regdev-then-comdev probe order, the gamemode-by-flag-name rule, the devparm-true global toggle, the hardcoded DEVDATA basename trio, the early-return short-circuit of the candidate scan, the basedefault overwrite to DEVDATA default.cfg, the absence of a vanilla -iwad flag, the -file PWAD-loader scope, and the M_CheckParm case-insensitive lookup), VANILLA_DEVELOPER_MODE_PARAMETERS (3 frozen entries: -shdev pins shareware/doom1.wad/index 0, -regdev pins registered/doom.wad/index 1, -comdev pins commercial/doom2.wad/index 2), VANILLA_NONEXISTENT_DASH_IWAD_PARAMETER_NAME='-iwad', VANILLA_DASH_FILE_PARAMETER_NAME='-file', VANILLA_DEVELOPER_MODE_DEFAULT_CFG_BASENAME='default.cfg', VANILLA_DEVELOPER_MODE_PARAMETER_COUNT=3, VANILLA_USER_SUPPLIED_IWAD_SELECTION_DERIVED_INVARIANTS (10 derived parity invariants), VANILLA_USER_SUPPLIED_IWAD_SELECTION_PROBES (13 pinned probes), and the REFERENCE_VANILLA_USER_SUPPLIED_IWAD_SELECTION_HANDLER frozen handler with crossCheckVanillaUserSuppliedIwadSelection and deriveExpectedUserSuppliedIwadSelectionResult helpers. The module imports only the GameMode type from src/bootstrap/gameMode.ts.

Playable-contract module declaring DEFAULT_IWAD_PATH='doom\\DOOM1.WAD', LEGACY_PACKAGE_START_SCRIPT='bun run src/main.ts', and PRODUCT_RUNTIME_COMMAND='bun run doom.ts' plus the verifyIwadDiscoveryAtLaunch() async function that probes the default IWAD path via Bun.file().exists() and returns a frozen VerifyIwadDiscoveryAtLaunchEvidence record with stepIdentifier='14-003' anchoring the launch-time IWAD-discovery proof. The evidence record contains a commandContract sub-record (acceptsOnlyProductCommand=true / legacyPackageStartScript / productRuntimeCommand / runtimeCommand), a discovery sub-record (defaultIwadPath / discovered=true / discoveryApi='Bun.file.exists' / fileContentsRead=false / sourceFile='src/main.ts'), a launchTransition sub-record (fromState='launch-command-accepted' / mapName='E1M1' / scale=2 / skill=2 / toState='iwad-discovered'), a replayCompatibility sub-record (discoveredBeforeFirstTic=true / inputStreamMutated=false / randomSeedMutated=false / simulationTicsAdvanced=0), and SHA-256 hashes pinning the discoveryHash and transitionHash sub-records computed via Bun.CryptoHasher over JSON.stringify of the sub-records. The function throws with a verbatim error string when the runtimeCommand is not the product command or when the IWAD file does not exist at launch time.

Playable-contract module declaring DEFAULT_LOCAL_IWAD_PATH='doom\\DOOM1.WAD' (the canonical Windows-style local-reference-bundle path the future bun run doom.ts entrypoint will probe by default) and IMPLEMENT_IWAD_DISCOVERY_CONTRACT (a frozen ImplementIwadDiscoveryContract record that pins the productRuntimeCommand 'bun run doom.ts', the legacy 'bun run src/main.ts' entrypoint, the help usage lines, the deterministic-replay-compatibility flags discoveryStage='startup-before-game-session' / frameDependent=false / gameStateMutation=false / replayInputDependency=false, the discovery commandLineParameter='--iwad', the defaultCandidate path+source 'local-reference-bundle', the missingDefaultResult='null-deferred-to-03-008' delegation marker, the provider 'Bun.file().exists()', the source 'Bun-runtime-entrypoint', the stepId='03-007', and the stepTitleSlug='implement-iwad-discovery'). Exposes the discoverIwadPath() async function that resolves IWAD path tri-state DiscoverIwadPathResult by source 'command-line' | 'default-local-reference-bundle' and status 'discovered' | 'missing'.

Playable-contract module declaring IMPLEMENT_MISSING_IWAD_ERROR_CONTRACT (a frozen MissingIwadErrorContract record that pins the canonical missingIwadError messageHeader 'Missing IWAD file for playable launch.', recoveryText 'Provide --iwad <path-to-iwad> or place DOOM1.WAD at doom\\DOOM1.WAD.', failureKind 'missing-iwad', defaultCandidatePath 'doom\\DOOM1.WAD', explicitArgument '--iwad <path-to-iwad>', reportsCheckedPaths=true, throwsBeforeWindow=true, schemaVersion=1, stepId='03-008', and the deterministic-replay-compatibility flags failureTiming='launcher-startup-before-session' / mutatesGameState=false / mutatesReplayInput=false / requiresWindowHost=false). Exposes formatMissingIwadError() that constructs the user-facing multi-line error string from a MissingIwadErrorInput { checkedPaths, explicitPath } record and createMissingIwadError() that wraps the formatted string in an Error.

plan_hash_evidence_documents.role: Plan-level pin document that fixes the shareware DOOM 1.9 IWAD as the single primary parity target and freezes the SHA-256 fingerprint, byte size, and on-disk relative path of every local artifact every later step is allowed to compare against. Pins the shareware DOOM1.WAD SHA-256 1D7D43BE501E67D927E415E0B8F3E29C3BF33075E859721816F652A526CAC771, the byte size 4196020, the lump count 1264, the two on-disk relative paths doom/DOOM1.WAD and iwad/DOOM1.WAD, the DOS executable DOOMD.EXE SHA-256 9D3216605417888D5A699FA3794E46638EB4CB8634501AE748D74F9C2CFAE37B with byte size 709753, the merged Windows executable DOOM.EXE SHA-256 5CA97717FD79F833B248AE5985FFFADBEE39533F5E0529BC9D7B439E08B8F1D2 with byte size 1893888, the shareware episode count 1 and episode-map list E1M1..E1M9, the shareware game description 'DOOM Shareware', the shareware identification rule (basename doom1.wad case-insensitive plus E1M1 lump present and E3M1/E4M1 absent), and the proprietary asset redistribution policy that bars publishing the IWAD or executables under any name. Cross-references src/reference/target.ts, src/bootstrap/gameMode.ts, reference/manifests/file-hashes.json, and the read-only doom/ and iwad/ drop locations.

Plan-level pin document that fixes the scope of the two user-supplied DOOM IWAD parity targets that sit beyond the primary shareware target: registered DOOM (DOOM.WAD with E3M1 present and E4M1 absent) and Ultimate DOOM (DOOM.WAD with E4M1 present). Pins the supplied IWAD basename 'DOOM.WAD', the two on-disk relative paths doom/DOOM.WAD and iwad/DOOM.WAD that users may drop a proprietary IWAD at, the case-insensitive basename match against doom.wad that resolves both registered and Ultimate targets to mission='doom', the registered game mode 'registered' / game version 'exe_doom_1_9' / episode count 3 (E1..E3), the Ultimate game mode 'retail' / game version 'exe_ultimate' / episode count 4 (E1..E4), the registered identification rule (E3M1 present and E4M1 absent), the Ultimate identification rule (E4M1 present), the local-availability statement that no registered or Ultimate DOOM.WAD exists at doom/DOOM.WAD or iwad/DOOM.WAD in this working tree, and the proprietary asset redistribution policy that bars bundling either DOOM.WAD copy. Pins no SHA-256 fingerprints because the registered and Ultimate IWADs are user-supplied only and never canonically locked to a single byte stream. Cross-references src/bootstrap/gameMode.ts, src/reference/target.ts, plan_vanilla_parity/SOURCE_CATALOG.md, and the gitignored doom/ and iwad/ user-drop directories.

iwad_search_invariants.playable_missing_iwad_message_header: Missing IWAD file for playable launch.
iwad_search_invariants.notes: The seven-candidate access(R_OK) probe order is the verbatim sequence in id Software linuxdoom-1.10 d_main.c IdentifyVersion. The three developer-mode parameters -shdev/-regdev/-comdev sit at the top of IdentifyVersion and short-circuit the candidate scan via early return when matched. Vanilla DOOM 1.9 does NOT recognise a -iwad <path> flag; -iwad is a Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 addition that this audit deliberately does NOT pin under vanilla-1.9 authority. The two project-local gitignored drop directories 'doom' and 'iwad' are the canonical user-drop locations for both shareware DOOM1.WAD and user-supplied registered/Ultimate DOOM.WAD copies; the playable default path uses Windows backslash 'doom\\DOOM1.WAD' because Windows is the only supported host (per CLAUDE.md). The vanilla literal probe basenames are lowercase ASCII; the on-disk basenames are uppercase ASCII; the case mismatch is invisible on case-insensitive Windows but would force a fallthrough to indeterminate on case-sensitive POSIX hosts, which is a property of the host filesystem rather than IdentifyVersion itself.

hash_evidence_invariants.notes: The three shareware target hashes (IWAD DOOM1.WAD, DOS exe DOOMD.EXE, Windows polyglot exe DOOM.EXE) are the only canonically-pinned IWAD-related SHA-256 fingerprints at plan_vanilla_parity scope; no fingerprint exists for the user-supplied registered or Ultimate DOOM.WAD because those IWADs are user-supplied only and never canonically locked. The same three hashes are cross-referenced inside src/reference/target.ts PRIMARY_TARGET (executableHash, dosExecutableHash, wadHash; already byte-level inventoried by the prior 01-019 step) and inside reference/manifests/file-hashes.json (already byte-level inventoried by the prior 01-019 step). This 01-020 inventory captures only the plan-document-level hash declarations to avoid double-counting; the source-level cross-references stay under the 01-019 contract.

boundary_status.notes: src/bootstrap/, src/playable/bun-runtime-entry-point/, src/playable/bun-launch-local-distribution-boundary/, and plan_vanilla_parity/ are writable workspaces under plan_vanilla_parity governance. The doom/ and iwad/ proprietary asset directories are read-only outside this inventory's read-only paths and are intentionally NOT enumerated; bundle-content cataloging belongs to the prior 02-001 oracle-lane catalog-local-reference-binaries-and-configs step. The playable DEFAULT_LOCAL_IWAD_PATH uses the Windows-only backslash form 'doom\\DOOM1.WAD' because Windows is the only supported host per CLAUDE.md; a future cross-platform runtime would need to extend the contract rather than mutate this constant. No proprietary id Software bytes are embedded in any of the inventoried source files or this inventory; every IWAD or executable is referenced by filename + SHA-256 fingerprint only.

implications: src/bootstrap/implement-iwad-discovery-order.ts is the canonical source-of-truth for the verbatim seven-candidate access(R_OK) probe order from id Software linuxdoom-1.10 d_main.c IdentifyVersion. Future plan_vanilla_parity steps that implement a runtime IWAD discoverer must cross-check against VANILLA_IWAD_DISCOVERY_PROBES via the REFERENCE_VANILLA_IWAD_DISCOVERY_HANDLER cross-check, and may not reorder VANILLA_IWAD_CANDIDATES nor add candidates to the seven-entry list without updating the audit ledger and this inventory snapshot in lockstep. Adding a Chocolate-Doom-style -iwad <path> override or a multi-directory search would be a deliberate divergence from vanilla 1.9 and must be pinned under a separate audit ledger.

src/bootstrap/implement-local-shareware-iwad-default-path.ts pins the lowercase-literal probe basename 'doom1.wad' versus the uppercase on-disk basename 'DOOM1.WAD' on the project's Windows-only supported host. Any future runtime that resolves the shareware default path must apply Windows case-insensitive resolution; a POSIX runtime would force a fallthrough to indeterminate even when DOOM1.WAD is on disk, which is a property of the host filesystem rather than IdentifyVersion itself. The two project-local drop directories 'doom' and 'iwad' are the canonical user-drop locations and any change must propagate to the proprietary-asset-non-redistribution-boundary pin.

src/bootstrap/implement-user-supplied-iwad-selection.ts pins the absence of a vanilla -iwad flag in DOOM 1.9 plus the developer-mode triple -shdev/-regdev/-comdev that short-circuits the candidate scan via early return. A runtime that recognises -iwad <path> at the bootstrap layer would be a Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 surface, NOT a vanilla 1.9 surface, and must be pinned under a separate audit ledger. The playable runtime contract at src/playable/bun-runtime-entry-point/implementIwadDiscovery.ts declares its own --iwad parameter; that surface is the bun run doom.ts product entrypoint contract, NOT a vanilla 1.9 IdentifyVersion clause, and the two surfaces must be kept distinct.

src/playable/bun-runtime-entry-point/implementIwadDiscovery.ts pins DEFAULT_LOCAL_IWAD_PATH='doom\\DOOM1.WAD' as the canonical Windows-style local-reference-bundle default path the future bun run doom.ts entrypoint will probe via Bun.file().exists() before running the game. The discoverIwadPath() tri-state DiscoverIwadPathResult delegates the missing-default failure mode to step 03-008 implement-missing-iwad-error, which is the boundary where the user-facing error is constructed via createMissingIwadError(). The IMPLEMENT_IWAD_DISCOVERY_CONTRACT.deterministicReplayCompatibility flags pin discoveryStage='startup-before-game-session' / frameDependent=false / gameStateMutation=false / replayInputDependency=false, which is the load-bearing replay-safety property that downstream parity tests depend on.

src/playable/bun-launch-local-distribution-boundary/verifyIwadDiscoveryAtLaunch.ts pins the launch-time IWAD-discovery proof at stepIdentifier='14-003' with PRODUCT_RUNTIME_COMMAND='bun run doom.ts' and produces SHA-256 hashes (discoveryHash + transitionHash) over the JSON-stringified evidence sub-records via Bun.CryptoHasher. The hash construction is internal to the verification module and is not a parity-target SHA-256; future steps that need a stable evidence anchor must compute their own hashes rather than alias these.

plan_vanilla_parity/pin-shareware-doom-one-point-nine-primary-target.md is the plan-level pin document that fixes the shareware DOOM1.WAD SHA-256 1D7D43BE501E67D927E415E0B8F3E29C3BF33075E859721816F652A526CAC771, the DOOMD.EXE SHA-256 9D3216605417888D5A699FA3794E46638EB4CB8634501AE748D74F9C2CFAE37B, and the DOOM.EXE SHA-256 5CA97717FD79F833B248AE5985FFFADBEE39533F5E0529BC9D7B439E08B8F1D2. These three hashes are duplicated inside src/reference/target.ts PRIMARY_TARGET (already byte-level inventoried by 01-019) and reference/manifests/file-hashes.json (already byte-level inventoried by 01-019); the four declaration sources MUST stay in lockstep. Adding a registered or Ultimate IWAD hash to the plan would diverge from the user-supplied-only contract pinned by plan_vanilla_parity/pin-user-supplied-registered-and-ultimate-iwad-scope.md, which deliberately pins NO SHA-256 fingerprint for those targets.

plan_vanilla_parity/pin-user-supplied-registered-and-ultimate-iwad-scope.md fixes the user-supplied-only redistribution policy for registered and Ultimate DOOM.WAD copies and pins doom/DOOM.WAD and iwad/DOOM.WAD as the two canonical drop locations. The registered identification rule (E3M1 present and E4M1 absent) and the Ultimate identification rule (E4M1 present) match Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 D_IdentifyVersion exactly and are implemented inside src/bootstrap/gameMode.ts. The three tier IWAD search the project supports — shareware DOOM1.WAD (primary target, locally-bundled), registered DOOM.WAD (user-supplied), Ultimate DOOM.WAD (user-supplied) — share the same two drop directories and the same case-insensitive basename match.

Because this inventory records on-disk SHA-256 fingerprints for every source module, every plan document, and every companion test, any structural rewrite of src/bootstrap/implement-iwad-discovery-order.ts, src/bootstrap/implement-local-shareware-iwad-default-path.ts, src/bootstrap/implement-user-supplied-iwad-selection.ts, src/playable/bun-runtime-entry-point/implementIwadDiscovery.ts, src/playable/bun-runtime-entry-point/implementMissingIwadError.ts, src/playable/bun-launch-local-distribution-boundary/verifyIwadDiscoveryAtLaunch.ts, plan_vanilla_parity/pin-shareware-doom-one-point-nine-primary-target.md, plan_vanilla_parity/pin-user-supplied-registered-and-ultimate-iwad-scope.md, or any of the eight companion tests will diverge from the captured snapshot and require a follow-up update to this inventory. The focused inventory test enforces that divergence by recomputing each fingerprint at test time against the live filesystem, so a stale inventory will fail rather than silently mask a declaration rewrite.

The doom/ and iwad/ proprietary asset directories are intentionally NOT enumerated by this inventory because they lie outside this step's read-only paths and contain proprietary id Software bytes that the redistribution policy bars from any committed file. plan_vanilla_parity Phase 02 oracle-lane work owns bundle-content cataloging (02-001 catalog-local-reference-binaries-and-configs, 02-002 verify-local-reference-file-hashes); the inventory lane work captured here owns only the source-level declarations ABOUT the IWAD search surface and the SHA-256 fingerprints that anchor IWAD identity.
